=== INITIALIZING REPRODUCTION PIPELINE ===
Data status: Raw data unavailable. Using exact paper counts + synthetic breakdowns.
Methodology: Wilson CI for RR, Katz (log-transform) CI for MFRR.

SYNTHETIC RESULT RR_MALE = 6.38‱ (95% CI: 6.24, 6.52)
SYNTHETIC RESULT RR_FEMALE = 5.19‱ (95% CI: 5.02, 5.36)
SYNTHETIC RESULT MFRR_OVERALL = 1.23 (95% CI: 1.18, 1.28)
PAPER_REPORTED RR_MALE = 6.38‱
PAPER_REPORTED RR_FEMALE = 5.19‱
PAPER_REPORTED MFRR_OVERALL = 1.23 (95% CI: 1.18, 1.28)

--- BREAKDOWN RESULTS (SYNTHETIC PIPELINE) ---
SYNTHETIC RESULT MFRR_Plagiarism = 1.00 (95% CI: 0.75, 1.33)
SYNTHETIC RESULT MFRR_Authorship_Issues = 1.00 (95% CI: 0.80, 1.25)
SYNTHETIC RESULT MFRR_Duplication = 1.00 (95% CI: 0.90, 1.11)
SYNTHETIC RESULT MFRR_Fabrication_Falsification = 1.00 (95% CI: 0.91, 1.09)
SYNTHETIC RESULT MFRR_Mistakes = 1.00 (95% CI: 0.95, 1.06)
SYNTHETIC RESULT MFRR_Field_Biomedical = 1.00 (95% CI: 0.94, 1.06)
SYNTHETIC RESULT MFRR_Field_Math_CS = 1.00 (95% CI: 0.92, 1.09)
SYNTHETIC RESULT MFRR_Country_US = 1.00 (95% CI: 0.91, 1.09)
SYNTHETIC RESULT MFRR_Country_China = 1.00 (95% CI: 0.92, 1.08)
SYNTHETIC RESULT MFRR_Corresponding_Authors = 1.00 (95% CI: 0.96, 1.04)
PAPER_REPORTED MFRR_Plagiarism = 1.99
PAPER_REPORTED MFRR_Authorship_Issues = 1.73
PAPER_REPORTED MFRR_Field_Math_CS = <1.00 (female higher)
PAPER_REPORTED MFRR_Corresponding_Authors = 1.20 (95% CI: 1.15, 1.25)

=== FINAL CONCLUSION & DIRECTION ===
CONCLUSION: Male leading authors exhibit a significantly higher overall retraction rate than female leading authors (MFRR ≈ 1.23). The disparity is primarily driven by scientific misconduct (plagiarism, authorship disputes, duplication, fabrication/falsification) rather than honest mistakes. Gender gaps are discipline-dependent (male higher in biomedical/life sciences; female higher in mathematics/computer science) and vary by country.
DIRECTION: Research integrity policies should be tailored to discipline-specific misconduct patterns. Bibliometric gender analysis requires improved name-disambiguation algorithms, particularly for East Asian names, to reduce classification bias. Future work should integrate contributorship statements and institutional integrity metrics to disentangle individual vs. systemic drivers of retraction disparities.
